findings.evidence: Lines 759-765 prove `hausdorff_bernstein_widder`; lines 770-775 repeat the same `hausdorff_bernstein_widder_of_exists_of_laplaceTransform` assembly.
findings.issue: `hausdorff_bernstein_widder_unique` rebuilds the non-unique HBW theorem instead of deriving from the already public `hausdorff_bernstein_widder`.

findings.evidence: `rg` hits show `Basic.lean:368` has `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone.of_isCompletelyMonotone`, `BernsteinTheorem.lean:768` has the general unique theorem, while lines 781-784 duplicate existence plus `laplaceTransform_unique`.
findings.issue: `existsUnique_representsLaplace_of_isCompletelyMonotone` reproves the strong-predicate unique-existence special case by combining existence and uniqueness manually, although the closed-half-line unique theorem already gives it.

summary: The main statements are at the right mathematical level, but two unique-existence corollaries duplicate already-proved general results instead of deriving the special/general follow-ons from them.
